title: ADR-0035: Go/TypeScript contract-client bridge
verdict_summary: Accepted ADR (not superseded/stub/pattern-source) — a family-wide seam-consumption mechanism that mandates real build work. It generalizes the proven orvex-studio-mcp codegen pattern into a family rule. Most per-satellite obligations ARE covered by existing codegen stories: mcp (E-S 'Contracts-tag codegen: fail-loud typed client + one deliberate /v1 regeneration'), ui (E1-S2 'Contracts codegen consumer + typed BFF /api client'), staging ('Contracts codegen wiring + AGPL-import denylist + drift gate'), workgraph ('Repo scaffold + six-tier layout + contracts-tag codegen'). Three obligations are uncovered: (1) the centralized contracts-owned codegen recipe + codegen.manifest.yaml — the load-bearing novelty of the whole ADR (§2 'What is centralized is the RECIPE, not the output') — which every per-satellite codegen story implicitly depends on but no contracts story builds; (2) orvex-studio-api's contracts codegen wiring — the one consuming satellite with NO codegen story, and precisely the one the ADR headlines as unblocking its two standing TODOs; (3) TS CloudEvent envelope/payload type generation via json-schema-to-typescript, which §6 explicitly frames as a distinct, independently-unblocked deliverable and which no TS story names.

gaps.requirement: AD-0035 §2 — contracts-owned centralized codegen recipe + codegen.manifest.yaml
gaps.quote: What is centralized is the RECIPE, not the output. The contracts repo ships one shared codegen script + a codegen manifest (`codegen.manifest.yaml`) that pins, for the whole family: the generator versions, the deterministic flag set (stable ordering, no timestamps), and — per consuming satellite — which authored artifact(s) are that satellite's legal sources. Each satellite invokes that shared recipe; config is centralized, output is distributed.
gaps.why_uncovered: No orvex-studio-contracts story builds a shared codegen script or codegen.manifest.yaml. The contracts plan covers SEAMS.md (E1-S3), versioning/x-status (E1-S4), the provenance guard (E1-S5), the OpenAPI/JSON-Schema authoring (E2-*), and the CloudEvent catalog (E3-S1) — none is the centralized generator recipe/manifest. Every per-satellite codegen story (mcp/ui/staging/workgraph) assumes 'invoke the shared recipe' but the shared recipe + manifest itself is unbuilt. This is the mechanism the ADR centralizes to remove config drift (the sole downside of per-repo generation); without it the family reverts to N divergent configs.
gaps.suggested_home: orvex-studio-contracts plan (new foundation story: shared codegen script + codegen.manifest.yaml pinning generator versions/flags/per-satellite legal sources)
gaps.verdict: REFUTED — owned as already-scheduled definition-phase work. issue-bodies/pack-contracts.md (the Wave-1 orvex-studio-contracts Definition Pack) AC5 is a binary, machine-checked gate requiring the pack to 'define the contract TAG naming scheme, cut the first tag, and wire TS-client generation per ADR-0035 into the tag pipeline (TS clients for api/mcp/ui, Go stubs for Go satellites)' with its own Tasks line 'Wire TS-client + Go-stub generation into the tag pipeline per ADR-0035; cut the first tag; confirm fixtures round-trip in contracts CI.' Contracts story E1-S4 additionally builds the byte-identical-from-tag determinism proof (TestCodegenReproducibleFromTag) and E1-S5 the provenance guard — together these are the substantive load-bearing behaviour the codegen.manifest.yaml artifact exists to pin (generator determinism, per-satellite legal sources, tag-pinned reproducibility). The literal filename codegen.manifest.yaml is not named, but that is an implementation detail of an already-assigned, machine-gated Wave-1 obligation, not an unowned requirement. bridge-proof.md (the ADR-0035 proof-on-one-seam issue) further confirms this pipeline is being stood up ahead of any service pack freezing. Not a surviving build gap.

gaps.requirement: AD-0035 §2 / §5 — orvex-studio-api contracts codegen wiring (retire the two standing hand-authored TODOs)
gaps.quote: orvex-studio-api — waiting on this decision. Its typed surfaces are hand-authored with explicit standing TODOs: `src/types/domain.ts` ("land once orvex-studio-contracts pins a TS codegen path") and `src/cards/card.ts` ("hand-authored typed surface until orvex-studio-contracts pins …"). It has no codegen wired. … `orvex-studio-api` unblocks its two standing codegen TODOs immediately.
gaps.why_uncovered: Of the consuming satellites, orvex-studio-api is the only one with no codegen story. Its foundation epic (E1-S1..S7) covers repo extraction, edge-auth, cell-guard, CloudEvent outbox/consume, persistence, and strangle sequencing — but no 'contracts codegen wiring' story; its typed clients (E8-S1 DocmostPort, E9-S1 entitlement) are engine-primitive clients, not the contracts-off-BFF codegen that retires domain.ts/card.ts. This is the exact obligation the ADR names as immediately unblocked, so it needs a BUILD story.
gaps.suggested_home: orvex-studio-api plan (foundation epic — 'contracts codegen wiring: generate DTO/domain types off pinned contracts, retire src/types/domain.ts + src/cards/card.ts TODOs, thin zod validator at ingress')
gaps.verdict: REFUTED — owned as already-scheduled definition-phase work. issue-bodies/pack-api.md AC8 ('TS-client bridge') is a binary machine-checked gate: 'Given orvex-studio-api is the one TS/Hono satellite, When the tag cuts, Then it emits a TS typed client per ADR-0035, resolving the open Go-vs-TS codegen seam (audit finding #5)... Assert: the tag pipeline generates a TS client for api; it compiles' — this is verbatim the domain.ts/card.ts-TODO-retirement obligation the gap describes, cross-referenced against ADR-0035 explicitly. Its Tasks section adds 'Land + git-TAG in orvex-studio-contracts; wire TS-client generation per ADR-0035 into the tag pipeline.' This is the api Service Definition Pack (Wave-3), which per bridge-proof.md's own dependency note is exactly where 'broader multi-seam codegen coverage is owned by each Wave-3 service pack, born FROM those packs' — i.e. the api pack will spawn the concrete build-prompt story once certified, not before. It is pre-story (blocked on the pack certifying), not unowned. Not a surviving build gap.

gaps.requirement: AD-0035 §1 / §6 — TS CloudEvent envelope + per-type payload generation via json-schema-to-typescript
gaps.quote: There is no CloudEvent type generation anywhere — the Go legs read the event schemas directly, but the TS consumers (mcp's revocation consumer, studio-api's `/internal/events` sink, ui via the BFF) have no generated envelope/payload types. … TS CloudEvent types (envelope + per-type payloads) are generated per §1 and land now as draft-sourced types … they do not wait on the unshipped orvex-studio-lib TS Kafka binding.
gaps.why_uncovered: The Go side is covered (lib 'pkg/events: generated event-type constants + payload types'), but no TS story generates envelope/payload types via json-schema-to-typescript off events/schemas/*.json + _envelope.json for the TS consumers. api E1-S5 ('CloudEvent consumption + idempotent event dedupe') and the mcp revocation consumer handle consumption but do not name TS event-type generation. §6 explicitly frames this as a distinct deliverable unblocked independently of the Kafka driver, so it should not be assumed folded into consumption wiring.
gaps.suggested_home: orvex-studio-api and/or orvex-studio-mcp plan (event-consumption epics — 'generate TS CloudEvent envelope + payload types via json-schema-to-typescript off pinned event schemas; ajv-off-schema validator at the trust boundary')
gaps.verdict: CONFIRMED — re-verified ADR-0035 (slug QbEBPuKcGR) live via docmost-cli: Status Accepted, not superseded; §6 'CloudEvent types unblocked independently of the Kafka driver' states verbatim 'TS CloudEvent types (envelope + per-type payloads) are generated per §1 and land now... they do not wait on the unshipped orvex-studio-lib TS Kafka binding' — an explicit, independently-actionable obligation. Exhaustive grep of feature-map.txt, every breakdown/*/plan.json covers[] and body file, and every issue-bodies/pack-*.md + bridge-proof.md for 'json-schema-to-typescript', 'CloudEvent' + type/generat/payload/schema combinations, and 'ajv' returns zero hits anywhere naming TS CloudEvent envelope/payload type generation. contracts stories (E3-S1..S6) only author the schemas/catalog/profiles (producer-side, Go-adjacent), never TS consumer codegen off them. api's E1-S5 (CloudEvent consumption) and mcp's revocation-consumer stories (E5-S1..S3) consume against pinned schemas with no type-generation AC. pack-api.md AC8 and pack-contracts.md AC5 both scope 'TS-client' generation to the OpenAPI/REST toolchain (openapi-typescript+openapi-fetch) only — ADR-0035 §1 itself treats REST and CloudEvent/JSON-Schema codegen as two distinct toolchains (json-schema-to-typescript + ajv), and no pack or story names the second toolchain at all. Genuine, currently-unowned build/definition gap — survives.

components.name: codegen.manifest.yaml (contracts-owned)
components.quote: The contracts repo ships one shared codegen script + a codegen manifest (`codegen.manifest.yaml`) that pins, for the whole family: the generator versions, the deterministic flag set (stable ordering, no timestamps), and — per consuming satellite — which authored artifact(s) are that satellite's legal sources.

components.name: shared codegen script (contracts-owned recipe)
components.quote: What is centralized is the RECIPE, not the output. The contracts repo ships one shared codegen script + a codegen manifest

components.name: openapi-typescript + openapi-fetch REST toolchain
components.quote: REST seams (OpenAPI): generate types only with `openapi-typescript`; call them at runtime through `openapi-fetch`. This is already the mcp toolchain and is the family standard for every TS consumer of an `openapi/*.yaml` document.

components.name: json-schema-to-typescript event-type generator
components.quote: CloudEvent / JSON-Schema seams: generate types with `json-schema-to-typescript` off `events/schemas/*.json` + `_envelope.json` (and the standalone `schemas/*.json` shapes).

components.name: ajv-off-schema runtime validator (trust boundary)
components.quote: Runtime validation at a trust boundary is done with ajv against the shipped JSON Schema — the schemas are already the SoT … so the wire validator is the authored schema itself, not a hand-copied mirror.

components.name: per-repo checked-in generated.d.ts trees
components.quote: Each TS satellite generates exactly the tags it consumes and commits the output (mcp's `generated.d.ts` model, generalized). The contracts repo stays a non-building seam repo: it ships no generated TS and no npm-published client artifact.

components.name: CI determinism gate (eng-1406-r2-regen generalized)
components.quote: A CI determinism gate (mcp's `eng-1406-r2-regen` generalized) re-runs codegen against the pinned source and fails if the checked-in output drifts.

components.name: contractsRelease + orvexContractsPin in each package.json
components.quote: Each consumer records `contractsRelease` (the git tag, e.g. `v0.1.3`) and an `orvexContractsPin` map (per-artifact commit SHA) in its `package.json`.

components.name: thin hand-authored zod validators at BFF/UI ingress
components.quote: Each replaced DTO keeps a thin hand-authored zod validator at the BFF/UI ingress that parses into the generated type (or, for the JSON-Schema-backed shapes, an ajv validator per §1).
